description_long: This house is a three-story structure with a steeply pitched, gray shingled hip roof and a prominent chimney. The front facade features a traditional half-timbered design with visible wooden beams and light-colored infill, as well as rows of symmetrically arranged windows with white shutters. The ground floor includes a series of arched openings, some of which appear to be passageways or entrances, and painted murals or decorative panels between the arches. The side and rear facades are plain, with minimal detailing and no visible windows, emphasizing the decorative focus on the front. The roof includes dormer windows and a small chimney, adding to the building's historic European character. The overall appearance suggests a blend of traditional German or Central European architectural styles, suitable for a residential neighborhood or historic urban district.
description: A three-story, half-timbered house with a steep hip roof, arched ground floor openings, and symmetrical window rows. The front is richly detailed, while the sides and rear are plain.
description_view_0: Front view showing half-timbered facade, symmetrical windows with shutters, arched ground floor openings, and decorative murals.
description_view_1: Side view revealing a plain, windowless wall and the steeply pitched roof extending to the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view with a completely plain, windowless wall and the roof's full slope visible.
description_view_3: Back or alternate side view with symmetrical window rows, plain facade, and arched ground floor passageway.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched ground floor openings with decorative murals, no visible porch, and a main entry likely under one of the arches.
